{{DISPLAYTITLE: Object 61: ''"Everbulbs"''}}__NOTOC__
'''Object 61:'''
''"Everbulbs"''
==Description== [[File:Everbulb 2.jpg|thumb|Picture of an Everbulb installed in someone's outpost in an unknown location. Photographer is unknown. ]] Everbulbs seem to resemble vintage light bulbs, with the opacity of its glass and amount of light emitted varying based on the location it is found in. Everbulbs seem to take the appearance of any other light bulb within the level. If the light bulbs in a level, or region of a level have a certain set of features, say, the glass encasing the mechanisms for the light bulb are more opaque, and the light bulbs are bigger than most, then the Everbulbs that may be found in the level will have the same physical features. Everbulbs are often rarer in levels where the light bulbs are higher quality and well-maintained. This effectively makes Everbulbs with clearer glass, that emit more light, a more rare and prized possession or collectible across [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]. These Everbulbs are, as previously somewhat mentioned, very rare to come across in [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]], and for good reason. The main, most glaring detail of these Everbulbs, are that they never go out. After many tests from [[M.E.G.]], it has been concluded that even if the filament inside is destroyed, every piece that formerly used to compose the filament, will still proceed to glow, unless a chemical reaction or process turns the metal composing the filament into a new substance. The crushed, powdered form of Everbulbs are also in market, but is sold far less, as it is somewhat more useless than an actual Everbulb. Everbulbs are also found rarely in some anomalous levels. If an Everbulb is found in an extremely chaotic or unstable level, the light inside and glass encasing the bulb will both develop swirly patterns, with the inside of the bulb seemingly having a swirly sort of glittery fog or liquid inside. Due to the rarity of the form of these Everbulbs, experimentation of them by [[M.E.G.]] has been forbidden. There are many names for these Everbulbs, including "Everswirls", "Galaxy Bulbs", "Whirlpool Bulbs", and the name formally recognized by [[M.E.G.]] and most other large organizations, '''''"Gyric Everbulbs"''''' (derived from the Greek word "Gyro", meaning rotation, or spiral.) . These Everbulbs are far more rare than the other aforementioned Everbulbs, and are highly valued by collectors and wanderers alike. ==Usage== As mentioned before, Everbulbs have a very useful property, that being that they do not ever go out or stop emitting light. This can be used in a variety of applications, with the most notable one being that it is useful in levels with a lack of light sources. Due to this, these are both extremely useful and valuable. They also emit a notable amount of heat, but not enough to inflict great damage on humans. The light an heat output of an Everbulb can be enhanced, and there are a few ways. One way, that was accidentally discovered a while back by a [[M.E.G.]] scientist was to leave in alone in an empty environment, for at least 24 hours. The effects were noticeable, as the Everbulb emitted far more light than before the accidental experiment. The light emitted by an Everbulb may also be enhanced if the bulb is used in an unstable level. The last, and firstly discovered method to enhance the light an heat emitted by an Everbulb is to simply connect it into a circuit with more than 82 watts of electricity flowing within it. This enhances the Everbulb for about a week until the Everbulb's light and heat capacity return to normal. Due to the extremely useful properties that Everbulbs posses, and their extreme rarity, along with somewhat of a quality system, these Everbulbs are highly valuable in trade across all of [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]. Not only are they used as tools by wanderers, but also collected by people who can afford them. It is not recommended to sell these if you find one, as their utility is extremely useful for those wandering [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]. However, there are those who collect and sell these at bulk, with the most prominent group to do this being The Scrap Barons. In fact, the Everbulb is the most collected and sold item in their database. These Everbulbs also have a unique property that seems to only apply for [[Level 3.53]] as of now. Whenever an ''The Alarms'' in the level go off, or the water reservoir sector floods, any Everbulbs currently in the level begin to flicker, and once the catastrophe hits, the bulbs glow a bright red or crimson. It is unknown if this is due to some underlying force behind ''The Alarms'' and the floods in [[Level 3.53]], although it is very possible, as no other explanations prevail. ==Locations== [[File:Level 3.53.jpg|thumb|An extremely rare find, two Everbulbs found in the same room in Level 3.53]] Everbulbs are essentially found in any level where light bulbs are prominent. However, Everbulbs are incredibly rare to come by, especially if they are higher quality. Everbulbs have been found in [[Level 0.7]], [[Level 3.53]], [[Level 11.6]], and many, many others. They are, as mentioned before, extremely hard to come by, however they seem to be found more commonly in [[Level 3.53]]. Cleaner, more professionally-maintained levels have higher quality Everbulbs, however they do seem to be rarer. Only 3 Everbulbs have ever been found in [[Level 0.7]], and they are all novelty items. Levels that have been found to have at least 1 Gyric Everbulb include [[Level 450]], the red sections of [[Level 11.6]], and the 2nd area of [[Level 20.1]]. Everbulbs are also sold in a verity of qualities by The Scrap Barons, and possibly other groups. These items are sold in large quantities by The Scrap Barons, and they barter them in a variety of levels. ==Variants== ===Object 61-A: Gyric Everbulbs=== As mentioned previously, Everbulbs have a very rare variant, only found in unstable levels. These are called Gyric Everbulbs and appear to have a swirling fog within the glass encasing, giving the Everbulb a far more mystical look. These Gyric Everbulbs are rumored to increase quantum fluctuations and no-clipping, however this claim has no solid evidence behind it. These are extremely rare, and are often very bright, giving it a very high value in the eyes of collectors. ===Object 61-B: Utility Everlights=== Utility Everlights are gadgets that take an Everbulb, and enhance its abilities, giving it more readily-available uses. These contraptions were commonly seen inscribed on blueprints found in [[Level 3.53]]. The blueprints show exactly how to make and craft one for yourself, and also includes what mechanisms do what. These Utility Everlights have various different and new features and enhancements that the normal Everbulb does not have. It does still utilize the Everbulb. It has various features, such as being able to control the cone of light that the Everbulb emits, being able to enhance and focus the light of the Everbulb to make a very hot ray of light that has the capability to melt lead and weld metals together, make flashes of light that have the capability of disorienting, scaring off, or blinding entities such as [[Entity 3|Smilers]], and being able to emit infrared and ultraviolet light. These abilities all have different and useful applications for anyone ranging from simple wanderers trying to navigate [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]] and not die, to engineers and welders residing in [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]], simply looking for something to enhance their craft. Most, if not all blueprints of these Utility Everlights have been confiscated or collected by The Scrap Barons in order to maintain a solid grip over the market for these Utility Everlights. This makes the easiest and most efficient way to obtain a Utility Everlight being to buy them from The Scrap Barons. If you do not want to spend your resources, it is still possible to find a blueprint scattered anywhere in [[Level 3.53]], but extremely rare, as you are, on average, far more likely to find 3-4 more Everbulbs before you ever find yourself a blueprint of a Utility Everlight. ==Extra Information== ===Theories=== There are a few theories on how the Everbulbs are able to emit their light indefinitely. One of these theories are the Light Feeder Theory. This theory states that the metal inside the Everbulb is in fact extremely unique, theorized to be a currently unknown isotope of tungsten that has yet to be man made. This tungsten is able to attract and feed on the photons around it. The tungsten then begins to overheat, releasing and attracting its own photons back into itself, creating an infinite loop. This somewhat explains the fact that Everbulbs seem to constantly appear, seemingly out of nowhere, as the light bulbs could simply have the tungsten inside them bumped up through some quantum or chemical reaction into this special isotope of tungsten, and then slowly feed on the surrounding photons until it absorbed too much light, and somewhat became "over-saturated" with light, making its photons leak back into the environment. The Scrap Barons personally seem to believe in The Light Feeder Theory, as it seems far more plausible to them. There is also the theory that these Everbulbs are powered in the same way that [[Level 3]] and [[Level 1]], and many other levels with anonymous and unexplainable sources of energy get their energy from. This theory is somewhat formally known as The Unseen Sun theory. It states that just like the some levels in [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]], the Everbulbs get their electricity through some unseen, telepathic source. The theory also states that unstable levels are levels far away from this "unseen sun", and due to that the light inside the Everbulbs become unstable, causing it to become swirly
